utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey bruce so see here my my notes here is you here he had positive lab work for hep c so how're you doing today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay but i'm a little bit anxious about having hep c i've really surprised because i've been feeling fine they had done it as you know a screen as just part of my physical so i'm really surprised that that came back positive

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so in the past have any doctors ever told you that you had hep c

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no never that's why i'm i'm so surprised

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so just you know i need to ask do you have a history of iv drug use or you know have known any hep c partners

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean i used to party a lot and even did use iv drugs but i have been clean for over fifteen years now

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay that that's good i mean i'm i'm happy that you were able to to kick that habit i know a lot of my patients that i see you know they're still dealing with with those dements so i'm i'm i'm happy that you're able to do that so hopefully we can get you better okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so what about alcohol use is that something that you used to do a lot

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did i did i mean i i still have a beer here and there everyday but not as much as i used to

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and have you ever smoked before

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do smoke i smoke about one to two cigarettes per day i've cut down a lot but i'm just having a hard time kicking those less too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah yeah and that that's something i've got to work on too because hep c along with smoking you know both of those are n't are n't good so hopefully we can help you out you know if your pcp has n't prescribe something for you already and possibly we can we can do that for you as well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so do you have any other medical conditions

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i'm actually other than that i just had my physical and i'm not taking any medications no i'm i'm pretty good otherwise

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and what conditions would you say run in your family

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i have high blood pressure diabetes and depression

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so let me go ahead and do a quick physical exam on you so i reviewed your vitals and everything looks good and on general appearance you appear to be in no distress no jaundice on the skin on your heart exam you have a nice regular rhythm rate

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: regular rate and rhythm with a grade two out of six systolic ejection murmur is appreciated on your lung exam your lungs are clear without wheezes rales or rhonchi on your abdominal exam bowel sounds are present your abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hepatosplenomegaly yes let me i will change that one

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: splenomegaly and on your muscle exam there is no gait disturbance or edema so i did we i was able to review your your results of your recent lab work and your hcv antibody test was positive so your your liver panel we did one of those and it showed an elevated ast at thirty nine but your alt albumin and total bilirubin were all within normal limits so that's pretty good so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and plan for you so you do have hepatitis c so your initial labs were consistent with that hep c diagnosis and so you know i do n't know if you read much about hep c but hepatitis c is a viral infection that does affect your liver and you've most likely had it for several years now it it it most patients do n't see symptoms until years later so the next step that i would like to do is just confirm the diagnosis with some additional blood work so that includes checking your hep c rna and your hcv genotype and i would also like to determine the severity of your liver disease by checking for fibrosis of the liver and we will do that by ordering an ultrasound elasto elastography with this information we will we we will be able to know how we can proceed as far as treatment right so how does that sound

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i hmmm so i do have a wife and kids so should i be worried about them

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay yeah so we can start with the same screening that you had for august first so we'll just let's do that hep c antibody test and i'll actually help you set up those appointments with your your family doctor and then we can just see you back in three weeks and based on the results you know we will take action as needed okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay that sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: my nurse will be in with those those orders

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright thank you

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright thanks

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: bye

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 60-year-old male presenting for follow-up of a positive hepatitis C antibody test.
Hepatitis C
The patient reports feeling anxious and asymptomatic regarding the positive hepatitis C screening result.
The doctor asks if the patient has a prior diagnosis of hepatitis C.
The patient denies any prior diagnosis of hepatitis C.
The doctor asks about a history of intravenous drug use or exposure to hepatitis C.
The patient reports a history of intravenous drug use, with the last usage occurring over 15 years ago.
The doctor asks about the patient's history of alcohol use.
The patient reports a history of heavy alcohol use and currently drinks beer occasionally.
The doctor asks about the patient's smoking history.
The patient currently smokes 1 to 2 cigarettes per day and reports difficulty with cessation.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other medical conditions.
The patient denies any significant past medical history or current medication use.
The doctor asks about the patient's family medical history.
The patient reports a family history of high blood pressure, diabetes, and depression.
The doctor declares that vital signs are normal, the patient is in no distress, there is no jaundice, and the heart has a regular rate and rhythm.
The doctor declares a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, and a soft abdomen with no hepatosplenomegaly.
The doctor reviews lab results showing a positive HCV antibody test, elevated AST at 39 U/L, and normal ALT, albumin, and total bilirubin.
Treatment plan
The doctor orders hepatitis C RNA and HCV genotype testing, and an ultrasound elastography to evaluate for liver fibrosis.
The patient asks if his family is at risk for hepatitis C.
The doctor advises screening the patient's family and scheduling a follow-up visit in 3 weeks to review results.
